name: Basic Muffins and Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease twelve 6 cm muffin cups. Preheat oven to 200°C (392°F).
2. In a medium-sized bowl, sift flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.
3. Stir the dry ingredients together.
4. Beat egg with a fork in a small bowl, then add milk and oil.
5. Mix the wet ingredients into the dry mixture until combined.
6. Pour batter from a tablespoon into each prepared muffin cup, filling them about 1/2 to 2/3 full.
7. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove muffins and serve hot with butter, jam, or marmalade.
Variations:
1. Ginger Muffins - Add 1/2 cup finely diced candied ginger to the flour mixture before adding liquid.
2. Banana Pecan Muffins - Use only 110 ml milk, add 60 g chopped pecans and 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg to the sifted flour, and mix in 1 cup mashed banana with the egg, milk, and oil.
3. Blueberry Muffins - Toss 1 cup washed and well-drained fresh or frozen blueberries with the flour mixture before adding liquid.
4. Orange Muffins - Cut 2 peeled navel oranges into sections. Place an orange section on top of each muffin before filling the cup the rest of the way.
5. Cheese Muffins - Fold 50 g grated sharp yellow cheese into the batter during the last few strokes and serve with scrambled eggs and bacon for a special breakfast.
6. Surprise Muffins - Fill muffin cups about 1/3 full, drop in a dollop of your favorite jelly, then fill the rest of the cup.
7. Coconut Muffins - Add 1 cup shredded coconut to the last few strokes of mixing. Serve with butter and milk for a snack.
8. Chive Muffins - Fold 1/4 cup chopped chives into the batter during the last few strokes and serve at dinner, great with steak and salad.
